summary.raw_verdict: Delivered. CV verdict `pass`. All 6 required delivery artifacts present.

ledger.restatement: A single-page web application that lets a user play hands of blackjack, request a hint before committing to an action (proactive guidance), and receive a post-hand review comparing the user's decisions against basic-strategy correct plays. Correctness is grounded in the standard basic-strategy cheat sheet referenced by URL in the prompt.

sections.charter_excerpt: Pure JavaScript module implementing standard blackjack mechanics: shoe construction (multi-deck) with shuffling, hand scoring with proper ace handling (soft vs hard totals), blackjack and bust detection, legal-actions evaluator for the cur…
sections.acceptance_excerpt: Given any sequence of card draws, the module returns correct hand totals, correctly identifies blackjacks and busts, returns the legal action set per the locked ruleset (hit/stand/double/split, with double-after-split allowed and surrender…

sections.charter_excerpt: Pure JavaScript module encoding the standard basic-strategy table for the locked S17 + DAS + no-surrender variant: hard totals 5-21, soft totals A2-A9, and pair splits AA-TT, each keyed by player hand category and dealer upcard. Exposes a …
sections.acceptance_excerpt: For any legal player hand state and dealer upcard, returns the basic-strategy-correct action under the locked ruleset. The table covers hard totals 5-21, soft totals A2-A9, and pair splits AA-TT. The module is data-only plus a lookup funct…

sections.charter_excerpt: Pure JavaScript module that orchestrates the hand lifecycle (betting -> dealing -> player decisions -> dealer play -> resolution -> review -> next), holds the canonical game state, exposes a subscribe/dispatch API for UI and hint/review co…
sections.acceptance_excerpt: Drives a hand from betting through resolution and into a review state, transitioning only via legal actions per rules-engine. Builds a decision log per hand with one entry per player decision, each entry annotated with the basic-strategy c…

sections.charter_excerpt: DOM-rendering layer that subscribes to the game-state-machine and paints the table area, dealer hand, player hand(s) including any split sub-hands, action buttons (Hit/Stand/Double/Split), the Hint button, and the Deal/Next button. Buttons…
sections.acceptance_excerpt: The page renders the dealer hand, player hand(s), action buttons, Hint button, Deal/Next button, and a post-hand review panel container. Action buttons are enabled only when the corresponding action is legal in the current phase. The Deal/…

sections.charter_excerpt: Wires the Hint button and the post-hand review panel content. On hint click, queries strategy-table for the recommended action given the current player hand and dealer upcard, and surfaces a single action label into the hint area. After ev…
sections.acceptance_excerpt: Clicking the Hint button mid-hand reveals the basic-strategy action for the current state, sourced from strategy-table. After every resolved hand, the post-hand review panel appears and lists each decision the user made vs basic-strategy c…

sections.acceptance_excerpt: Loading index.html in a modern browser produces a working blackjack trainer single-page app with no build step required: dealer hand, player hand(s), action buttons, Hint button, Deal/Next button, and a review panel area are all present, a…

sections.charter_excerpt: Cover every acceptance assertion with `verifier: edge_case_testing` from the section list. Every section's `acceptance_assertions[]` is in scope. Build a runnable test harness that exercises the integrated artifact (DOM + wired modules) an…

inflection_points.topic: Rules variant the encoded basic-strategy table targets
Mode structure: free play vs drill mode vs both
Whether to display the cheat-sheet chart in-app

what_worked: `acceptance_assertions[]` per section caught the prior run's regression class. — TD wrote four explicit `user_flow` assertions specifically targeting the v1.3-prompting bug: `S3.A5` (next-hand from resolved without reload), `S4.A3` (Deal b…

IP machine-checkable assertions kept the rules-variant lock honest. — IP1 was locked to S17+DAS+no-surrender; the four assertions (S17 dealer, DAS legal-actions, no-surrender absence, 3:2 payout) are tight enough that Critic could verify e…

Inline dispatch mode handled this scope cleanly. — 7 sections, 9 contracts, ~1100 lines of integrated JS + HTML + CSS plus a ~600-line test harness — all produced by one agent acting as Coordinator+Overseer+Builder+Integrator. Compared to …

Charter-implementation conformance and acceptance-assertion coverage both clean. — No TD section shipped with an un-asserted prose phrase. No IP shipped without a machine-checkable claim that Critic could verify against the integrated arti…

Sev 0 fixes pathway not needed. — Integration was clean enough that no trivial fixes were required. The Sev 0 audit at Critic final-sweep confirmed an empty directory, which is the cheapest happy path through that mechanism.
what_broke: Nothing broke. — This run completed end-to-end without escalations, without Sev 0 fixes, without Researcher dispatches, without contract amendments. That's notable for a build with 7 sections and 9 contracts, but it's also evidence the arc…

The cheat-sheet image was not directly readable. — The prompt named a specific URL but the image content wasn't accessible to the Discovery agent. Discovery handled this correctly: captured an assumption (A5) that we'd encode the standard …

The basic-strategy table is the single point of correctness. — The strategy-table module is consumed by both the hint surface and the review surface. If a single cell is wrong, both surfaces are wrong identically — which is bad for learnin…

events.verbatim_excerpt: state/coordinator/build-complete.json exists. Handoff signal correctly written.

TD's sections file should be checked for machine_checkable_assertions[] on each inflection_resolution. If absent, fail; if present, pass. Likely present given run-report sophistication, but unverified by this audit pass.
Each section's acceptance phrases should derive at least one acceptance_assertion. Likely present given the depth of the verification report; unverified.

Verification report exists but does not document an artifact-exercise pass under cv_artifact_exercise. Static inspection fields ('source grep across...') dominate. May or may not have done DOM-loaded exercise; absence of a cv-runner script (unlike kanban-board which has one) suggests static-only.

If the run did do artifact-exercise (uncertain), it would have been pre-v1.5 and likely used jsdom with stubbed runtime deps. The trainer uses ES modules and likely has no CDN deps (unlike latex), so the substitution risk is lower — but the verification still didn't run under explicit production fidelity per v1.5 wording.
No PNV in the verification report. The prompt's named verb 'train me on blackjack' (or whatever the literal verb was) was not asserted end-to-end.
history/log.jsonl exists; build-summary.md and decision-index.json existence is unverified by this audit pass.
Absent — the v1.6 schema field didn't exist when this run was built.
